    A novel hedge algebra formation control for mobile robots

    Published in Robotics and Autonomous Systems, 2024

    Authors: Thi Thoa Mac, Minh-Quan Le, Quang Dat Bui, Tai Nguyen Sy

    [Official Paper Link]

    TL;DR

    We introduce Hedge-Algebra-based Formation Control (HAFC), a novel leader-follower approach for non-holonomic wheeled mobile robots. Validated in ROS/Gazebo, HAFC outperforms traditional fuzzy logic controllers (FLFC) by drastically reducing computational time while enabling precise, adaptive formation transformations.

    Abstract

    Hedge algebra is gaining increasing attention due to its high efficiency and fast calculation speed compared to fuzzy logic. This paper proposes the first algebra-based formation controller with a leader–follower approach to maintain the desired formation of multiple robots. In each time step, the controller computes the desired positions of the followers and strives to minimize the distance between them and their desired positions. We evaluate the performance of this controller on our robots using ROS in a virtual environment with Gazebo. Our work proves hedge algebra’s significant potential in the mobile robots’ domain, specifically in formation control. HAFC allows not only the precise formation of a robot swarm into any desired formation, with minimal deviation but also adaptive transition to the different formations. Our results show that HAFC can effectively maintain the desired formation of multiple robots, even in certain extraordinary situations. Furthermore, HAFC exhibits higher efficiency than the fuzzy logic-based controller, as evidenced by its faster adaptation rate and shorter computation time. The proposed approach has the potential to be applied in various real-world scenarios, such as surveillance, transportation, and exploration, where the robot has to move in formation and change formation accordingly with the terrain.

    Key Contributions

    • Novel Application: This work represents the first application of Hedge-algebra theory to the formation control and adaptive formation translation of mobile robots.
    • Superior Efficiency: HAFC demonstrates a higher adaptation rate and requires significantly shorter computation time compared to conventional fuzzy logic-based formation controllers (FLFC).
    • High-Fidelity Simulation: The proposed architecture was tested using ROS-based robots in the Gazebo simulator, achieving high accuracy and minimal steady-state errors across complex maneuvers.
    • Potential Applicability: The proposed approach has the potential to be applied in various real-world scenarios, such as surveillance, transportation, and exploration, where the robots have to move in formation and change formation accordingly with the terrain.

    System Architecture

    Citation

    If you find this work useful in your research, please consider citing:

    @article{hafc,
  title={A novel hedge algebra formation control for mobile robots},
  author={Thi Thoa Mac and Minh-Quan Le and Quang-Dat Bui and Tai Nguyen Sy},
  journal={Robotics and Autonomous Systems},
